text.title: schedule-development Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for schedule-development as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No dependencies defined — every task appears to run in parallel on the plan.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Parallel-looking schedules hide sequential constraints. When a dependency causes a delay, the team learns about it at the dependency boundary, not during planning when it could have been mitigated.

failure-mode.description: Optimistic single-point estimates — every task is the best-case duration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Projects almost never run at best-case. A schedule built on optimistic estimates has no slack for real-world variance, so the first delay propagates immediately to the end date.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring the critical path — treating all tasks as equally important.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Equal management attention on all tasks means critical tasks get insufficient focus. The project finishes late on the path that received the least attention.

failure-mode.description: No buffers — the schedule shows tasks back-to-back with zero slack.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A single delay anywhere on the critical path breaks the end date with no recovery option. The PM must re-negotiate scope or dates under pressure, in public, in crisis mode.

failure-mode.description: Resource conflicts — the same person is scheduled on parallel tasks simultaneously.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Theoretical parallel execution that ignores per-resource capacity produces a plan that is physically impossible. Resource leveling must be applied before baseline commitment.
